creator: University of Guelph Library
description: The library of University of Guelph digitizes 1,247 documents from the 1987 Archaeological Survey—correspondence, newspapers, manifestos. Without proper DC cataloging, these primary sources remain invisible to historians, journalists, and students researching history.

Historical Impact: A journalist researching Canada's discoveries in 1987 finds personal letters from a government official—primary sources that reshape her article. A high school teacher finds the manifesto collection for students. A historian cites 47 documents in her book. All because proper DC metadata enabled discovery.
provenance: Original documents in UoG Library archives. Digitization 2016-2017.
rights: Public domain; see individual item metadata
subject: 1987 Archaeological Survey
title: 1987 Archaeological Survey Digitized Document Collection
Alternative Title: Digitized 1,247 documents from the 1987 Archaeological Survey
